qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via script test mode injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a code injection vulnerability in the /api/runscript endpoint using script test mode and Module._compile(), which is a different vulnerability than the advisory-described authorization bypass for scheduler creation/modification. Different root cause (unsafe code compilation vs missing authorization), different vulnerable endpoint (/api/runscript vs scheduler endpoints), different CWE (code injection vs CWE-862 missing authorization), and different security impact (RCE vs unauthorized scheduler manipulation).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-c869-jx4c-q5fc) describes an authorization bypass vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to create and modify arbitrary schedulers (CWE-862: Missing Authorization). The single qualifying finding describes a code injection/RCE vulnerability in the /api/runscript endpoint via script test mode, which is a distinct vulnerability with different root cause, vulnerable code, endpoint, and security impact. While both vulnerabilities involve unauthenticated access in the default configuration, they affect different components (scheduler management vs script execution) and have different exploitation mechanisms.
evidence: Advisory describes: 'authorization bypass vulnerability allowing unauthenticated, remote attacker to create and modify arbitrary schedulers'
Advisory affected component: scheduler creation/modification endpoints
Finding describes: 'code injection vulnerability in the script execution endpoint' via /api/runscript
Finding vulnerable code: server/runtime/scripts/msm.js Module._compile() for script test mode
Different root causes: missing authorization check vs unsafe dynamic code compilation
Different security impacts: unauthorized scheduler manipulation vs arbitrary code execution
